raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California Air Resources Board (CARB) halted their planned increase to gas prices through an amendment of the Low Carbon Fuel Standard fee.",
    "Senator Rosilicie Ochoa Bogh stated that a gas price hike of 65 cents or more would deepen financial strain on families in California.",
    "Republican senators submitted 13,000 petition signatures to CARB asking them to delay the vote on the gas price hike.",
    "AAA reported that as of February 19, the average price for a gallon of gas in California was $4.85, well above the national average of $3.16.",
    "Senate Minority Leader Brian W. Jones described the proposed gas price hike as 'price gouging at the pump.'",
    "The Newsom Administration's 65-cent gas price hike was paused by California's Office of Administrative Law because it did not meet legal requirements.",
    "Senate Minority Leader Brian W. Jones claimed that the regulation failed to accurately warn Californians about the true impact it would have on gas prices.",
    "Senate Minority Leader Brian W. Jones alleged that Newsom's plan aimed to increase gas prices to force consumers into buying expensive electric vehicles (EVs).",
    "Senate Bill 2 was introduced to repeal the regulation and overturn the 65-cent gas price hike."
  ]
}
```
